senses.definition: to gradually slow down and come to a complete stop

examples.x: The traffic on the highway began to grind to a halt as a result of a major accident.
The meeting started smoothly but eventually ground to a halt due to disagreements among team members.
The economic downturn caused many construction projects to grind to a halt.

senses.definition: to accelerate very fast, particularly so that smoke can be seen coming out from the tires

examples.x: The drag racer revved the engine and burned rubber as he sped down the track.
In his excitement to leave, he accidentally burned rubber in the driveway.
The getaway car burned rubber as the bank robbers made a quick escape.
The enthusiastic driver burned rubber at the start of the race, aiming for an early lead.
The teenager wanted to impress his friends, so he burned rubber in the parking lot, leaving tire marks behind.

senses.definition: to have a tendency to drive very fast

examples.x: Watch out for the driver in the fast lane; he's known to have a lead foot.
She tends to have a lead foot when running late for appointments.
The police officer pulled him over for speeding, noting that he appeared to have a lead foot.
My friend claims to be a cautious driver, but he can't deny he has a lead foot when he's on the open highway.
The racing enthusiast admitted he has a lead foot on the track but is mindful of speed limits on regular roads.

senses.definition: to take the front passenger seat in a vehicle such as a car or truck

examples.x: I called dibs to ride shotgun on the road trip; I want the front seat.
The kids argued over who would get to sit shotgun during the family drive.
She always insists on sitting shotgun when we carpool to work.
The friends took turns riding shotgun on the way to the concert to share the best view.
The older sibling allowed the younger one to sit shotgun for the first time during the drive to the beach.

senses.definition: a car, particularly one that is very desirable or expensive

examples.x: I just got my driver's license and can't wait to get my own set of wheels.
Borrowing a friend's set of wheels made the weekend getaway much more convenient.
Getting a reliable set of wheels was a priority for the commuter who relied on public transportation.
The teenager worked a part-time job to save up for a set of wheels to have more independence.

senses.definition: to take a vehicle for a brief, leisurely ride

examples.x: On a sunny afternoon, they decided to take a spin around the countryside in their convertible.
Feeling restless, she suggested they take a spin through the city to enjoy the evening lights.
The friends borrowed bikes and decided to take a spin along the scenic coastal trail.
He offered to let her take a spin in his new sports car to experience its speed and handling.
After a busy day, taking a spin on her motorcycle was the perfect way for her to unwind.

senses.definition: to state that one will be sitting in the seat next to the driver in a vehicle

examples.x: Before the road trip, everyone rushed to call shotgun for the best view.
He called shotgun as soon as they mentioned going out for a drive.
The siblings had a standing agreement that whoever called shotgun first got the front seat.
When the group decided to order takeout, she called shotgun to ride along and pick up the food.
The friends laughed as they playfully argued about who called shotgun first for the ride to the concert.
